title: Training Methodology (Enablement Pattern)
description: A training methodology is a process for upskilling a LEARNER (human or autonomous agent) through EXPERIENCE — workshops, practice, drills, mentoring. It raises the capability of an actor; it never changes model weights (that is the methodologies collection). Each pattern is located on the Craft Path (Step) and against orthogonal facets. Pure data, no code. Sibling to methodologies.schema.json; shares the catalog-wide id namespace.

id.description: Stable kebab-case id. Shares the catalog-wide id namespace; must not collide.

aliases.description: Other names in the wild, including original-language terms.

kind.description: foundation = pre-Operator culture layer; move = atomic teachable unit; track = orchestrated multi-Step program; guardrail = cross-cutting safety lens.

step.description: Which Step(s) of the Craft Path this pattern serves.

facets.description: Orthogonal coordinates locating the pattern in design space.

container.description: workshop | build-clinic | bootcamp | cohort-course | hackathon | dojo | leadership-retreat | coaching | async | embedded

craft.description: AI Fluency 4D: delegation | description | discernment | diligence; plus literacy-basics

learner.description: Who is upskilled. Defaults to human; autonomous-agent for agent-learner Moves.

summary.description: Two to four sentences in plain, simple English: what this Move produces (a more capable learner), when it pays off, and how it differs from adjacent Moves.

intent.description: One sentence: what running this Move accomplishes.

when_to_apply.description: Preconditions and triggers — when to reach for this Move.

inputs.description: What must be in place before running (learners, facilitator, a real task, tools, time).

outputs.description: What the Move produces — above all a more capable learner, plus any Masterpiece artifact.

steps.description: Ordered procedure of the session/program. Each step names a sub-activity in plain English.

threshold.description: The test that earns the next Step — how you know the learner is ready to advance.

masterpiece.description: The demonstrable artifact that proves the Step was earned.

known_uses.description: Real-world programs that exemplify this Move (official courses, company experiments). Carried over from the candidate todo.

known_failure_modes.description: Anti-patterns this Move is vulnerable to when applied carelessly.

related.description: Other training pattern ids that combine, precede, succeed, or contrast.

unlocks_methodologies.description: Methodology categories a graduate of this Move/Step is now equipped to execute (the link to the methodologies collection).

diagram.description: Mermaid diagram showing how inputs flow through the session steps to a more capable learner. Mandatory.

type.description: Diagram kind; drives the leading Mermaid keyword (flow -> 'flowchart TD').

mermaid.description: Mermaid source, no fences. First non-blank line begins with the keyword matching type (e.g. 'flowchart TD' for flow).

example_scenario.description: Concrete worked walkthrough: who is training whom, what inputs they had, how the steps played out, what came out, what they learned. Mandatory — a Move without a worked example collapses into abstract advice.

deep_dive.description: Optional fuller treatment for practitioners. Primary fields stay plain English; rigour and detailed trade-offs live here. Markdown allowed.
